popularStory.headline: My Maternal 19th. Great Grandmother, Lady Jean or Joan Campbell
popularStory.description:
Name: Lady Joan Campbell
Born: 1295 in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland
Married: about 1318 in Argyll, Scotland to Sir John Mor Lamont, 3rd. Laird of Clan Lamont
Children: (6)
Mary Isabella Lamont 1318–1364 …

zh.Lochowe, Argyllshire, Scotland: 英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛霍
zh.Kilfinan, Argyll, Scotland: 蘇格蘭阿吉爾吉爾分南
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Mor Lamont 3rd Chief of Clan Lamont於大約1290年出生在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾洛霍時，他的父親Malcolm Lamont 2nd Chief of Clan Lamont41歲，母親Lady Christina Macdougall26歲。他和Joan Campbell至少有2個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1353年享年64歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭阿吉爾吉爾分南。

zh-hans.Kilfinan, Argyll, Scotland: 苏格兰阿吉爾吉爾分南
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Mor Lamont 3rd Chief of Clan Lamont于大约1290年出生在英国苏格兰阿吉爾洛霍时，他的父亲Malcolm Lamont 2nd Chief of Clan Lamont41岁，母亲Lady Christina Macdougall26岁。他和Joan Campbell至少有2个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于1353年享年64岁，埋葬在苏格兰阿吉爾吉爾分南。

nameMeanings.name: Lamont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: from the medieval personal name Lagman, which is from Old Norse Lǫgmathr, composed of the elements lǫg, plural of lag ‘law’ (from leggja ‘to lay down’), + mathr ‘man’ (genitive manns).
French: habitational name, with fused definite article l', from any of the places called Amont, in Haute-Saône and Haute-Vienne.
Altered form of French   Lamothe  .

variants.name: McClammy
meanings: Scottish: patronymic from a pet form of   Lamont  .

variants.name: McClymont
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Laomuinn ‘son of Laomann’, a personal name borrowed from Old Norse and meaning ‘law giver’. See Lamont .

variants.name: Lemond
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lamont  .
French: variant of Lemonde, a nickname from Old French monde ‘pure’, with fused masculine definite article le.
French (Lémond): variant of Laymond, itself a variant, with fused definite article l', of Aymond .

variants.name: Lemont
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lamont  .
French: habitational name from Le Mont in the Vosges or possibly other minor places so named from Old French mont ‘mountain’. Alternatively, a variant of Lemond 2.

variants.name: Lemonds
meanings: Scottish: variant of Lamont , with English excrescent -s.

variants.name: Lawman
meanings: Scottish and northern Irish: variant of   Lamont  .

English: occupational name from Middle English lagman, lagheman ‘law-man, one whose duty is to declare the law; lawyer’ (Old Norse lagmann-, lǫgmann-, oblique case of lǫgmathr). The word was also widely used as a personal name, Middle English Lagman, Lagheman.

variants.name: Lamond
meanings: Scottish, northern Irish, and French: variant of   Lamont  . This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Lamothe
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Andre, Jacques, Pierre, Donat, Francois, Lucien, Odette, Adrien, Andree, Armand, Armande.
French: variant of   Lamotte  , or a habitational name from any of the places in France named Lamothe or La Mothe.

History: The settlement that became the city of Detroit was founded in 1701 by Antoine de la Mothe, Sieur de Cadillac (1658–1730), governor of LA. He was born into the minor nobility in Gascony, France, where his father owned the seigneury of Cadillac.

variants.name: Lamonte
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Lamont  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

nameMeanings.name: Mor
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Traditional name from mór ‘large, great’. This was the commonest of all girls’ names in late medieval Ireland, and has continued in use in both Ireland and Scotland to the present day.

en.lifeSummary: When John Mor Lamont 3rd Chief of Clan Lamont was born about 1290, in Lochawe, Argyll, Scotland, United Kingdom, his father, Malcolm Lamont 2nd Chief of Clan Lamont, was 41 and his mother, Lady Christina Macdougall, was 26. He had at least 2 sons and 3 daughters with Joan Campbell. He died in 1353, at the age of 64, and was buried in Kilfinan, Argyll, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John Mor Lamont 3rd Chief of Clan Lamont est né environ 1290, à Lochawe, Argyll, Écosse, Royaume-Uni, son père, Malcolm Lamont 2nd Chief of Clan Lamont, avait 41 ans et sa mère, Lady Christina Macdougall, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 3 filles avec Joan Campbell. Il est décédé en 1353, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Kilfinan, Argyll, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als John Mor Lamont 3rd Chief of Clan Lamont um 1290 in Lochawe, Argyll, Schottland, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Malcolm Lamont 2nd Chief of Clan Lamont 41 und seine Mutter Lady Christina Macdougall 26. Er hatte mit Joan Campbell mindestens 2 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1353 im Alter von 64 Jahren und wurde in Kilfinan, Argyll, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John Mor Lamont 3rd Chief of Clan Lamont è nato il circa 1290 nel Lochawe, Argyll, Scozia, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Malcolm Lamont 2nd Chief of Clan Lamont, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, Lady Christina Macdougall, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 3 figlie con Joan Campbell. Morì nel 1353, all'età di 64 anni, e fu sepolto a Kilfinan, Argyll, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: John Mor Lamont 3rd Chief of Clan Lamontが約1290年にイギリススコットランドアルグイルロチャウで生まれた時，父Malcolm Lamont 2nd Chief of Clan Lamontは41歳，母Lady Christina Macdougallは26歳であった。彼はJoan Campbellとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1353年に64歳で死去し，スコットランドアルグイルキルフィナンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John Mor Lamont 3rd Chief of Clan Lamont(이)가 대략 1290년 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 럭아베에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Malcolm Lamont 2nd Chief of Clan Lamont(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Christina Macdougall(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Joan Campbell(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1353년 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 아그이 킬핀아에 묻혔다.

mn.Lochowe, Argyllshire, Scotland: Lochawe, Argyle, Шотланд, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John Mor Lamont 3rd Chief of Clan Lamont nasceu aproximadamente 1290, em Lochawe, Argyll, Escócia, United Kingdom, seu pai, Malcolm Lamont 2nd Chief of Clan Lamont, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, Lady Christina Macdougall, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 3 filhas com Joan Campbell. Ele faleceu em 1353, com 64 anos, e foi sepultado em Kilfinan, Argyll, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John Mor Lamont 3rd Chief of Clan Lamont родился около 1290 г. в местности лочав, аргилл, Шотландия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Malcolm Lamont 2nd Chief of Clan Lamont было 41 год, а матери, которую звали Lady Christina Macdougall, 26. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 3 дочери от Joan Campbell. Он умер в 1353 г. в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности килфинан, аргилл, Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John Mor Lamont 3rd Chief of Clan Lamont nació aproximadamente 1290, en Lochawe, Argyll, Escocia, Reino Unido, su papá, Malcolm Lamont 2nd Chief of Clan Lamont, tenía 41 años y su mamá, Lady Christina Macdougall, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 3 hijas con Joan Campbell. Murió en 1353, a la edad de 64 años, y fue sepultado en Kilfinan, Argyll, Scotland.
